generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (PMSM) paired with cycloidal gear transmission for joint motion in MABI MAX 100 robot, enabling precise positional control through torque amplification and speed reduction."
]

instance CycloidalGearMechanism : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gear mechanism integrated with PMSM for motion transmission, providing mechanical amplification of torque while mitigating backlash through dual encoder configuration."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution position encoder mounted directly on PMSM shaft measuring angular displacement with sub-millimeter precision for real-time motion tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionData
]

instance GearboxPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary position encoder positioned behind cycloidal gearbox measuring angular displacement to mitigate backlash effects and ensure motion accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxPositionData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]

instance GearboxPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxPositionEncoder
]

instance JointFrictionSensorData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Aggregated sensor data combining motor and gearbox position measurements for friction identification and motion compensation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder, GearboxPositionEncoder
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance CycloidalGearFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model representing friction dynamics in cycloidal gear mechanism using multi-physics simulation (Simulink) for real-time friction identification and compensation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkFrictionAnalysis
]

instance PMSMMotionControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model of Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor control system implemented via SysML state machines (v1.7) for torque profile optimization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MotionControlEnabler
]

instance JointHealthMonitoringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Predictive model analyzing friction patterns and joint health using historical sensor data traces for anomaly detection and maintenance alerts."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HealthMonitoringEnabler
]

instance MotorPositionDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw angular displacement data from motor encoder transmitted via MQTT for real-time motion analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkFrictionAnalysis
]

instance GearboxPositionDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw angular displacement data from gearbox encoder transmitted via MQTT for backlash compensation analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxPositionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkFrictionAnalysis
]

instance JointFrictionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed friction identification data combining motor/gearbox position measurements with simulated friction models for control parameter adjustment."
    DTDFVocab:fromData JointFrictionSensorData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MotionControlEnabler, HealthMonitoringEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SimulinkFrictionAnalysis : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink multi-physics simulation environment processing position data to identify and compensate for joint friction in real-time."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionCompensationService
]

instance MotionControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML state machine-based control workflow implementing dynamic torque profile adjustment for motion accuracy optimization."
    DTDFVocab:enables MotionAccuracyOptimizationService
]

instance HealthMonitoringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics engine analyzing friction patterns to detect mechanical degradation and generate maintenance alerts."
    DTDFVocab:enables JointHealthMonitoringService
]

instance MQTTDataStreaming : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Lightweight MQTT protocol implementation for real-time sensor data transmission from physical components to Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionCompensationService, MotionAccuracyOptimizationService
]

instance AssetAdministrationShell : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Industry 4.0 compliant Asset Administration Shell standardizing data exchange and semantic interoperability for heterogeneous system components."
    DTDFVocab:enables JointHealthMonitoringService
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionCompensationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Active multi-physics-aware joint friction identification service dynamically adjusting control parameters to mitigate friction-induced motion errors."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCompensationInsight, TorqueProfileAdjustmentAction
]

instance MotionAccuracyOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive motion accuracy optimization service continuously refining control parameters based on real-time friction analysis for enhanced robotic precision."
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionAccuracyImprovementInsight
]

instance JointHealthMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time joint health monitoring service detecting friction anomalies and mechanical degradation through pattern analysis of sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:provides HealthAlertInsight, MaintenanceRecommendationAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionCompensationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time friction compensation insight providing dynamic adjustment parameters for torque profiles to minimize motion errors."
]

instance TorqueProfileAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of torque profiles based on friction analysis to optimize motion accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MotionAccuracyImprovementInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive insight quantifying expected motion accuracy improvements through friction compensation strategies."
]

instance HealthAlertInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Anomaly detection insight identifying abnormal friction patterns indicative of mechanical degradation."
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated maintenance recommendation based on joint health analysis to prevent equipment failure."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: Environment
instance MABIMAX100RobotEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Industrial robotic environment for manufacturing and assembly tasks requiring high-precision motion control."
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, CycloidalGearMechanism, MotorPositionEncoder, GearboxPositionEncoder, CycloidalGearTransmission, SiemensSINUMERIK840DController, MotionControlSystem
]

// C5: Components
instance CycloidalGearTransmission : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Mechanical transmission system converting motor torque to precise linear/rotary motion with minimal backlash through dual encoder configuration."
    base:isContainedIn MABIMAX100RobotEnvironment
]

instance SiemensSINUMERIK840DController : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller processing motion commands and integrating with Digital Twin for closed-loop control optimization."
    base:isContainedIn MABIMAX100RobotEnvironment
]

instance MotionControlSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Integrated control system combining PMSM, cycloidal gear, and encoder feedback for high-precision robotic motion execution."
    base:isContainedIn MABIMAX100RobotEnvironment
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents emphasizes **real-time, data-driven synchronization** between physical and digital representations, characterized by **live-data linking** between the physical object and its digital counterpart. While the document does not specify quantitative synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, ms), it implies **temporal alignment** is critical for the digital shadow's operational validity, as it relies on **continuous data aggregation and abstraction** from contextual traces. Behavioral modeling tools like **SysML state machines** and **MathWorks Simulink** are integrated to describe system dynamics, necessitating **temporal granularity** for accurate simulation and predictive analytics. However, explicit latency requirements or service-specific time scales (e.g., control loops, monitoring intervals) are not detailed in the text."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a **modular, component-based architecture** defined by **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as SysML IBDs, AADL, or MontiArc, which describe systems via **components, connectors, and composition**. Behavioral orchestration is achieved through **state machines** (e.g., SysML v1.7) and **MontiArc Automaton**, enabling **automated workflows** for data processing and system behavior modeling. The document does not explicitly detail **system-wide coordination mechanisms** (e.g., orchestration patterns, resource management, or plug-and-play integration), but it implies **integration of heterogeneous tools** (e.g., Simulink for detailed behavior) to achieve cohesive system operation. The **digital shadow** itself is described as consisting of **several data series**, suggesting a structured aggregation of contextual traces for unified representation."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, encompassing multiple abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. These stages are governed by methodologies such as **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)**, **OOSEM**, **SPES**, or **SMArDT**, which systematically decompose system complexity. Transition management between stages involves **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, which define components, interfaces, connectors, and composition. Behavioral modeling is further refined using **SysML state machines (v1.7), MontiArc Automaton, or simulation tools** (e.g., **MathWorks Simulink**) integrated with general-purpose languages (e.g., **C++/Java**). Lifecycle governance ensures alignment across heterogeneous stakeholders and perspectives, leveraging **interoperability frameworks** to unify disparate modeling tools and notations. The lifecycle is iterative, with continuous refinement from abstract requirements to executable technical models, ensuring traceability and consistency across phases.

Digital Shadows, as a precursor to DTs, are characterized by **one-way data aggregation** (contextual data traces) for specific purposes, lacking autonomous operation or feedback mechanisms. Their lifecycle is implicitly embedded within DT development but remains passive, relying on external systems for interpretation and processing.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** for this Digital Twin system is primarily defined by **MQTT-based communication** for streaming sensor data from physical assets (e.g., a Siemens CNC controller). The document does not specify **network topology, bandwidth requirements, or latency constraints**, nor does it mention **reliability mechanisms (e.g., redundancy, failover)** or **security protocols (e.g., TLS, encryption)**. While the broader context implies reliance on **industrial communication protocols**, only **MQTT** is explicitly referenced. The system’s connectivity is constrained to the **one-way data flow** characteristic of a digital shadow, lacking bidirectional or control-oriented interactions.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system leverages **software tools** such as **MathWorks Simulink** and other simulation environments for behavior modeling, alongside **general-purpose languages (C++, Java)** for implementation. Deployment infrastructure is not explicitly described, but the system integrates **visualization dashboards** for monitoring and analysis. Instead, the focus is on **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., SysML, ADLs) and **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** across modeling notations."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with **heterogeneous modeling tools and general-purpose languages**, including **SysML**, **MathWorks Simulink**, and **code in C++/Java** (Section II.A), enabling cross-tool interoperability for comprehensive system description. Data exchange is facilitated via **MQTT** (Section IV.A), a lightweight publish-subscribe protocol for streaming real-time data between physical and digital components. Additionally, the abstract mentions **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)**, a standardized interoperability framework (e.g., under **Industry 4.0** paradigms) for integrating Digital Twins with enterprise systems. The system also references **interoperability standards** implicitly through the use of ADLs (e.g., AADL, SysML), which define standardized interfaces and connectors for component-based engineering. However, specific integration patterns with other Digital Twins or detailed dependencies on external systems (e.g., ERP, MES) are not elaborated in the provided documents."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) system adheres to **industry standards** and **interoperability frameworks** primarily through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies and **architecture description languages (ADLs)**. Key standards include:
- **SysML (Systems Modeling Language, v1.7)** for **functional/logical architecture** and **behavioral modeling** (state machines).
- **AADL (Architecture Analysis & Design Language)** for **component, connector, and interface specification**.
- **MontiArc** for **model-driven architecture evolution** and **automaton-based behavior**.
- **ACME** for **generic component modeling** in distributed systems.
Compliance frameworks are inferred via **toolchain integration** (e.g., **MathWorks Simulink, AADL compilers**), ensuring adherence to **ISO/IEC standards** (implicitly referenced in MBSE practices). Interoperability is achieved through **unified modeling notations** (e.g., **SysML IBDs**) and **stakeholder alignment mechanisms**, though explicit **certification requirements** or **formal compliance frameworks** (e.g., **IEC 62264, ISO 15926**) are not detailed. The system prioritizes **heterogeneous tool interoperability** over rigid standardization bodies.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The provided documents do not explicitly describe cybersecurity protocols, access control mechanisms, or fail-safe mechanisms for the Digital Twin system. However, in **Document [42]**, the concept of a 'safety cell' is mentioned in the context of robotic automation, indicating a physical safety measure for the MABI robotic arm (referenced in **Document [44]**) to prevent collisions and ensure operator safety. No technical details regarding digital security (e.g., OAuth 2.0, X.509, or AAS security submodels) or safety-critical system constraints (e.g., RAMI 4.0 safety layers) are explicitly outlined in the provided documents. The focus of the referenced materials is primarily on model-driven engineering, digital shadow implementation, and friction modeling for robotics, without addressing cybersecurity or data governance frameworks."
]
